varieties.name: ‘Babylon’ (Napoleon Willow)
varieties.origin: China, introduced to Europe 1730 via Aleppo, Syria; 290+ year cultivation history
varieties.traits: Female clone; tree to 9-15m; long pendulous branches; yellowish-brown branchlets (not golden)
varieties.availability: Daley’s Fruit Tree Nursery (NSW), Flemings Nursery, Garden Express Australia, Parklands Australia

varieties.name: Salix × sepulcralis ‘Chrysocoma’ (Golden Weeping Willow)
varieties.origin: Germany, late 1800s; hybrid S. babylonica × S. alba ‘Vitellina’
varieties.traits: Most widely planted weeping willow cultivar globally; 12-15m tall × 12-15m wide; vigorous growth; golden-yellow young branches
varieties.availability: May be available through specialty Australian nurseries (check import restrictions)

varieties.name: ‘Crispa’ (syn. ‘Annularis’, Ram’s Horn Willow)
varieties.origin: Mutation of ‘Babylon’, documented 1838 by Loudon; 185+ year cultivation
varieties.traits: Female clone; medium tree 9-12m; upright open habit; slower growth than typical weeping willows; sterile (non-invasive)
varieties.availability: Rare in Australia; may require special order from international suppliers

varieties.name: ‘Tortuosa’ (Corkscrew Willow, Dragon’s Claw Willow)
varieties.origin: China; introduced to Arnold Arboretum USA 1923; entered commerce 1930s
varieties.traits: Upright tree 6-9m tall × 3-5m wide; TWISTED contorted branches (corkscrew effect); female clone; fast-growing
varieties.availability: Check with Queensland specialty nurseries; also marketed as Dragon’s Claw or Rattlesnake Willow

varieties.name: ‘Pendula’
varieties.origin: Original weeping willow selection from China; ancient cultivar spread along Silk Road trade routes
varieties.traits: Strongly pendulous branches and twigs; silvery sheen to foliage
varieties.availability: May be available through specialty Australian willow suppliers

varieties.name: ‘Niobe’ (Niobe Golden Weeping Willow)
varieties.origin: Modern cultivar; possibly S. alba × S. babylonica or S. × sepulcralis selection
varieties.traits: 15m tall × 15m wide; fast-growing; golden-yellow weeping branches; wide-spreading crown
varieties.availability: Check availability with Australian importers; popular in international markets

varieties.name: Salix tetrasperma (Indian Willow, Walunj) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Nepal, Laos, Thailand; indigenous to Kerala and southern India
varieties.traits: Medium-sized deciduous tree of wet and swampy places; sheds leaves at end of monsoon season (adapted to tropical cycle)
varieties.availability: Kerala Forest Department nurseries, local agroforestry suppliers, traditional plant markets in Thiruvalla region, State Forest Research Institute
